dataset: OME Open Microscopy Environment DeltaVision Microscopy Sample

Task description mentions ~200-5000 µm³ range; typical mammalian nucleus ~100-500 µm³ per BioNumbers; metadata sets minimum at 100 µm³ which aligns with biological expectations

Task metadata; verifier.py shows expected range 50-10000 µm³ for calibrated measurements; max of 5000 µm³ corresponds to ~17 µm diameter sphere, larger than typical somatic cell nucleus (~6-10 µm) but plausible for certain cell types
Task metadata; verifier.py comment states 'Uncalibrated (pixels) would be huge (>100,000)' and applies 10000 threshold for detecting uncalibrated measurements

pi_items.verified_value: OME (Open Microscopy Environment) DeltaVision sample repository; URL structure verified as OME legitimate format
pi_items.source: install_fiji.sh line 195; OME is real organization providing public microscopy data per bio-formats documentation

pi_items.verified_value: DV - real microscopy format based on MRC file format, contains spatial calibration metadata
pi_items.source: Bio-Formats documentation confirms DV format used for Applied Precision DeltaVision systems; supports wavelengths, dimensions, and spatial calibration

pi_items.verified_value: DAPI - 4′,6-diamidino-2-phenylindole, standard nuclear stain for fluorescence microscopy
pi_items.source: Filename DAPI_NUC indicates DAPI staining; confirmed by search results on DAPI fluorescence for nuclear analysis

pi_items.metadata_value: Must verify unit is microns not pixels (from task description)
pi_items.verified_value: DV files from DeltaVision systems contain embedded spatial calibration metadata; TIFF output format preserves calibration; task verifier expects measurements in µm³ (50-10000) vs pixels (>100000)
pi_items.source: Task description line states 'Verify the image properties to ensure spatial calibration is present (unit should be microns, not pixels)'; Bio-Formats documentation confirms DV format contains calibration; ImageJ/Fiji spatial calibration documentation

pi_items.verified_value: 3D Objects Counter v2.0 - standard Fiji plugin for 3D segmentation and volume measurement
pi_items.source: Fiji documentation and ImageJ plugins database confirm 3D Objects Counter provides thresholding, volume, surface area, and shape measurements

privileged_info_summary: The task uses a real DeltaVision DV format microscopy image (DAPI_NUC.r3d_D3D.dv) from the Open Microscopy Environment public repository. The file contains a 3D Z-stack of a DAPI-stained cell nucleus with embedded spatial calibration (in microns). Fiji's 3D Objects Counter plugin is used to segment and measure the nucleus. Expected measurements: volume 100-5000 µm³ (calibrated), >100,000 pixels (uncalibrated). Output includes CSV measurements and TIFF 3D object map. Data is real, not synthetic, sourced from OME public microscopy samples.
